steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main idea of the passage. The passage describes: (1) past studies hypothesized PSP was homologous (shared ancestor); (2) Vieira et al. examined common-ancestor fossils and found the bone openings were inconsistent with PSP; (3) the team concluded PSP evolved independently in each clade, contradicting the homology hypothesis.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Vieira et al. found openings in bones in fossils of common ancestors of pterosaurs and two other Cretaceous clades, but the team determined that the sizes of the openings were too varied to be indicative of PSP, thereby weakening the homology hypothesis.’: Misstates the opening-size evidence and omits the key conclusion that PSP likely evolved independently. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Although past studies by paleontologists concluded that PSP in pterosaurs and two other Cretaceous clades was likely attributable to a common ancestor, those paleontologists lacked access to technology that led Vieira et al. to reach a different conclusion.’: The passage never mentions a technology gap between past researchers and Vieira et al.; this reason is invented. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Previous studies hypothesized that PSP originated in a shared ancestor of pterosaurs and two other clades, but the findings of Vieira et al. suggest that PSP evolved independently in pterosaurs and was subsequently inherited by the other two clades.’: Directly contradicts the passage. The conclusion is that PSP evolved independently in each clade, not that it evolved once and was then inherited. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Vieira et al. found that pterosaurs and two other Cretaceous clades with PSP likely lacked a common ancestor with the trait, leading the team to posit a different evolutionary origin for PSP than that posited in earlier studies.’: Captures both the specific finding (no shared ancestor with the trait) and the broader implication (a different origin story than earlier studies proposed). ✓
That confirms D is correct.
